items.title: Ottawa approves Enbridge's $4B Sunrise natural gas pipeline expansion project
items.summary_auto: Federal Natural Resources Minister Tim Hodgson says the government has approved Enbridge Inc.'s $4-billion Sunrise project in B.C.

items.title: Ticketmaster delists Ontario resale tickets after omnibus budget bill passed
items.summary_auto: Ticketmaster Canada is delisting resale tickets that customers have posted for Ontario events as it prepares to comply with incoming provincial legislation.

items.title: Housing minister says Liberal ridings aren't favoured for funding after Gladu comment
items.summary_auto: Speaking earlier this month to the Sarnia Observer about her decision to leave the Conservatives and join the Liberal party, Gladu was asked whether she expected any rewards for bolstering the government's ranks.

items.title: Judge to release decision in sex assault trial for ex-Kitchener, Ont., neurologist Jeffrey Sloka
Syrian authorities arrest suspected leader of notorious Tadamon massacre
items.summary_auto: Syrian authorities said Friday they have arrested a former intelligence officer who appeared in a video leaked four years ago that purportedly showed him and his comrades fatally shooting dozens of people during the country's conflict.

items.title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs, Microsoft to offer voluntary buyouts for thousands
items.summary_auto: Meta is laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10 per cent of its workforce, the company said Thursday as it continues to ramp up spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and highly paid AI-expert hires.

items.title: N.B. child still waiting for dental surgery after two years
items.summary_auto: New Brunswick's only board-certified pediatric dentist says a lack of operating-room time has pushed his waiting list to more than 300 children, and some of them wait up to three years for surgery.

items.title: His language was lost. His worker was drunk: Inside Kirby Mistenapeo's time in care
items.summary_auto: The lawyer for the family of deceased Innu teen Kirby Mistenapeo says child protection officials in Newfoundland and Labrador were aware that his primary caregiver was drinking on the job — one of the many alleged systemic failings raised on Thursday.

items.title: Canada’s top general tries to reassure Ukraine as NATO tensions flare
Indigenous ancestral remains were found 20 years ago. Ontario says new homeowners have to investigate
Family says mom of baby who died was ‘begging for help’ at remote Ontario hospital, waiting for doctor's call
items.summary_auto: The family of an 11-month-old Cree baby from Attawapiskat who died in a Fort Albany hospital in northeastern Ontario this month says he would still be alive if he had received the health care he needed and deserved. Ontario’s Office of the Chief Coroner says it's investigating.

items.title: Mounties, border officers and cyber spies shut out of early retirement incentive
items.summary_auto: Front-line security and intelligence workers including Mounties won't be able to access the government's penalty‑free early retirement incentive as federal public safety agencies focus on getting more people in the door — not out.

items.title: Michael Jackson biopic turns up the music and mutes the controversy. What's wrong with that?
items.summary_auto: Antoine Fuqua's Michael is a surface-level replay of the iconic King of Pop's life. Audiences get toe-tapping entertainment value and an impressive impersonation by nephew Jaafar Jackson at the expense of virtually any investigation into the mind and motivations of the eponymous star.

items.title: Why Trump's appearance at White House correspondents dinner is triggering controversy
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump, who regularly attacks news coverage and journalists he dislikes with lawsuits and verbal tirades, will be the featured guest at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Association dinner, and annual gala celebrating freedom of the press.

items.title: Saros is an exhilarating sci-fi shooter set in a haunting, cryptic world
items.summary_auto: Finnish studio Housemarque follows up from its 2021 hit Returnal with Saros, refining the "bullet ballet" action to make it a more approachable game without sacrificing its adrenaline-pumping nature.

items.title: #TheMoment a Canadian won the biggest rodeo in the world
items.summary_auto: Jacob Gardner tells The National about the moment he became the first Canadian to win the bull-riding championship at the world's biggest rodeo in Houston.

items.title: Body of Canadian woman slain in Teotihuacán shooting returned to family
items.summary_auto: The body of a Canadian tourist killed when a gunman opened fire from one of Mexico's most visited pyramids has been turned over to her family, Mexican officials confirmed.

items.title: U.S. soldier involved in Maduro capture accused of profiting from knowledge of raid on Polymarket
items.summary_auto: A U.S. special forces soldier involved in the military operation to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro has been charged with using classified information about the mission to win more than $400,000 in an online betting market, federal officials announced Thursday.

items.title: Early-season wildfires in B.C. prompt warnings
items.summary_auto: B.C. Wildfire Service officials had said that the warm and dry winter experienced by most of the province could lead to heightened risk in some areas this summer.

items.title: At Issue | Carney fires back at Trump’s trade demands
items.summary_auto: At Issue this week: Ottawa responds to the Trump administration demands for trade concessions, including lifting the U.S. liquor ban. Mark Carney and Pierre Poilievre release duelling social media videos. And the Liberals move to control House committees with their newly-secured majority.

items.title: Coastal B.C. First Nation leaders go to Calgary to dissuade pipeline investors
items.summary_auto: A delegation of First Nations leaders from British Columbia have come to Calgary to relay a message to pipeline executives face-to-face: steer clear of investing in a new bitumen pipeline to the northwest coast or risk a prolonged legal fight.

items.title: Flood alert issued for Peace River, Alta.; residents told to prepare for possible evacuation
items.summary_auto: The Town of Peace River issued a flood alert later Thursday afternoon, warning residents of the northern Alberta community to prepare for a possible evacuation “on very short notice.”

items.title: Assembly of First Nations call for withdrawal of Red River Métis self-government bill
items.summary_auto: The Assembly of First Nations is calling for the withdrawal of a federal government bill that would recognize the right to self-government of the Red River Métis, in its second reading in the House of Commons.

items.title: Couple accused in international kidnapping case used fake Canadian camping trip as cover story: prosecutors
items.summary_auto: A couple from the United States is facing international kidnapping charges after prosecutors claimed they took a 10-year-old child to Cuba to potentially undergo gender transition surgery, allegedly deceiving the child's biological mother with a fake Canadian camping trip to get there.

items.title: Alberta pipeline could receive federal financing through Indigenous loan guarantee program: energy minister
items.summary_auto: Canada's energy minister said public dollars could flow to Alberta's potential bitumen pipeline to the Pacific Coast through the federal government's Indigenous Loan Guarantee program.

items.title: MrBeast's production company sued over alleged sexual harassment
items.summary_auto: A former Beast Industries employee is suing MrBeast's media production company after she was allegedly fired from her social media manager job upon returning from maternity leave and following years of what she described as sexual harassment and workplace gender bias.

items.title: Lebanon and Israel agree to extend ceasefire for another 3 weeks, Trump says
items.summary_auto: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to extend a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah by three weeks after talks at the White House on Thursday, according to U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: High stakes ahead for PWHL's super Saturday regular-season finale
items.summary_auto: All eight teams are in action in the PWHL's final day of the regular season on Saturday. The outcomes will determine which teams secure the final playoff spot, top seed in the playoffs and first-overall pick in the PWHL Draft.

items.title: Canadian astronaut Joshua Kutryk announced as part of a crew heading to the International Space Station
items.summary_auto: Hot off the heels of the Artemis II mission around the moon that included Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen, another Canadian has been announced for another space mission.

items.title: This video of Boston Marathon runners helping a man cross the finish line will heal you
items.summary_auto: When Ajay Haridasse's legs gave out within sight of the Boston Marathon finish line, two other runners sacrificed their personal bests to help him finish the race.

items.title: Dean Penney gave police an exact location to search for Jennifer Hillier-Penney’s body. It came up empty
items.summary_auto: The RCMP made six missions to search for Jennifer Hillier-Penney's body in the waters near Hare Bay Islands off St. Anthony, N.L., and searched over 130 points of interest. In his testimony in Corner Brook Supreme Court on Thursday, RCMP Cpl. Steven Hatch said the searches came up with nothing.

items.title: Toronto police want to talk to ex-cop who alleged antisemitism, corruption in senior ranks
items.summary_auto: Toronto police officials say they are reaching out to a high-ranking former officer who this week made explosive allegations of antisemitism, racism, and corruption in the service's senior ranks in a series of interviews surrounding the release of his new book.

items.title: Monstrous octopus terrorized seas off B.C. in Age of Dinosaurs, study suggests
items.summary_auto: Whale-sized, monstrous octopuses prowled the seas during the Age of Dinosaurs, snatching prey with their huge tentacles and crunching them with powerful jaws, fossils from B.C. and Japan suggest.

items.title: Firefighters heard 'stop, stop, stop' before deadly LaGuardia crash but didn't know warning was for them: NTSB
items.summary_auto: The National Transportation Safety Board has published its preliminary report on last month's deadly crash involving an Air Canada jet and a fire truck at New York's LaGuardia airport, concluding communication failures and a lack of transponders in the truck played roles in the collision.

items.title: Canadian drama Heated Rivalry wins prestigious Peabody Award
items.summary_auto: Crave's hit series Heated Rivalry has won a Peabody Award.

items.title: Teens used bear spray in Pokémon card robbery, say Vancouver police
items.summary_auto: Vancouver police say they arrested a trio of teenagers this week who allegedly used bear spray to steal two high-value Pokémon cards during a Facebook Marketplace transaction.

items.title: Want 5-minute EV charging? Canada is getting a megawatt charger, too
items.summary_auto: In this issue of our environmental newsletter, we look at a technology to charge an EV in five minutes; check out the beaches in Florida and the stinky problem they face this year; and hear the stories of three heroes of conservation in honour of Earth Week.

items.title: Police arrest 3 people in cybercrime investigation, seize ‘SMS blasters’ used to defraud victims
items.summary_auto: Police say an SMS blaster mimics “a legitimate cellular tower” that sends fraudulent text messages to connected phones with links to websites designed to steal personal, financial or log-in information.

items.title: Israeli strikes, fire kill 5 in Gaza and West Bank, medics say
items.summary_auto: Israeli airstrikes killed at least four Palestinians in Gaza on Thursday, and Israeli soldiers shot and killed a 15-year-old during an army raid in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, Palestinian health officials said.
